description: Helm rendering proves appliance temporal-worker uses 1,000 PBKDF2 iterations while the hosted/default temporal-worker remains at 10,000.
DB-backed happy path resets exactly the original tenant admin, verifies the new password through the normal application verifier, rejects the old password, and leaves unrelated users unchanged.
DB-backed guard cases for missing and ambiguous targets fail without modifying any user hashes.
Host-service requests require an authenticated management session and reject invalid passwords, mismatched confirmation, browser-supplied target identity, and concurrent active reset Jobs.

Generated Kubernetes resources use the current Alga image, correct application configuration, bounded lifetime, and cleanup while containing no plaintext password in names, arguments, labels, logs, status, or responses.
Status UI displays only the original admin identity and covers validation, confirmation mismatch, busy, success, and generic failure behavior.
Appliance smoke test resets the original admin through the authenticated management UI and signs into Alga with the replacement password.
